126090.explanation: GB has both female and male alderman candidates. More importantly, a candidate list of about 50 people led by two women and followed by 48 women and men, coming from all corners of the municipality of Voorst, and a strong connection to the local community life.

126091.explanation: It is extremely important that elected representatives (council members) weigh the opinions of residents in their decisions. GB is convinced that referenda are often used to amplify apparent divisions (polarization) on issues in society.

126093.explanation: When processing a permit application, the municipality makes a consideration of interests to enable a (social) initiative. GB believes that the costs for this should basically be borne by the applicants. Social initiatives can be an exception to this.

126094.explanation: GB is very positive about farmers who want to further develop their businesses. At the same time, a development for one can be a threat to the operations of another. GB is wary of uncontrolled growth of initiatives in our rural area, which is managed by our active farmers.

126097.explanation: All law-abiding parties will approach this statement positively. All municipalities are obliged under the Spreading Act to assist with reception. GB would like to bring this discussion back to the human scale, focusing on qualitatively good reception with respect for the environment.

126098.explanation: GB believes that everyone deserves a roof over their head. Seasonal work is mostly done by migrant workers (which is less the case in Voorst). Housing for these migrant workers should not come at the expense of the regular housing supply.

126102.explanation: New Year's Eve will be different after this last turn of the year. As GB, we believe that if there is a ban, an alternative must be offered. We see possibilities to organize a municipal fireworks show. Traditionally, we retain carbide shooting under conditions.

126103.explanation: Nothing comes for free. GB has long used the motto: The polluter pays! Ultimately, everything must be paid from the tax money we collectively raise. It is fair, decent, and above all most sustainable if everyone pays for their own waste.

126104.explanation: Festivals and parties foster a sense of unity, connection, and above all, enjoyment. LenteLive, the Klompenfeest, carnival, and tent parties are good examples. GB believes we should cherish this as long as there is no structural and excessive noise nuisance.

126110.explanation: GB supports stimulating music education. GB finds it important that every child has the same opportunities to develop talents (e.g., making music). GB wants to quickly identify unequal opportunities and intervene, for example with the help of Stichting Leergeld.

126112.explanation: Voorst is blessed with many monumental buildings. Owning a monumental property comes with certain duties, and we must not tamper with the associated rights. After all, everyone knows a place where once stood a (monumental) building, now regretted for demolition.

126113.explanation: "Decency must be practiced." It starts with children and parents. GB believes we should hold each other accountable for behavior. We choose prevention: employing youth workers and neighborhood teams to guide and prevent problems instead of enforcing.

126114.explanation: GB does not want to punish (repressively) through camera surveillance but rather deploy youth workers (preventively) to solve underlying issues at the root. Using cameras does not prevent our young people from ending up in Youth Care, which is more expensive afterward.

126119.explanation: Holiday parks are designed for short-term use. Permanent residence in a holiday park leads to changes in the park's composition, risks social problems, extra pressure on roads and/or criminal activities, and causes it to lose its recreational function.

126120.explanation: Most infill locations in the municipality of Voorst have now been largely utilized, and space must remain for greenery in the streetscape (climate adaptation). Moreover, the housing shortage calls for the construction of larger expansion areas. GB has therefore made 290 homes possible in Twello-Noord.

126121.explanation: Everyone deserves to participate appropriately in society according to their abilities and opportunities. Doing meaningful (volunteer) work plays an important role in this. Moreover, people stay actively involved, develop skills, and contribute to society.

126123.explanation: We must prevent residents from falling below the poverty line. GB believes raising an income threshold is not the solution for often other underlying problems. GB wants to invest in a good quality social foundation in which residents can develop themselves.

126125.explanation: Under GB's leadership, after the adoption of the Mobility Plan, the streetscape has changed, with an increase in public charging points for electric cars. GB wants to continue to facilitate this as needed. This must be done balanced as there is also high regular parking pressure.

126126.explanation: In a democracy, the majority respects the minority. Nevertheless, the quality of care is paramount. Care professionals can engage in dialogue to accommodate beliefs without abandoning professional standards.

126127.explanation: It is a legal obligation to tender Youth Care. These tenders must balance price/quality, with GB advocating for care close to home. When issuing tenders, GB wants to focus on small lots so that local providers can participate.

126128.explanation: The honest truth is that an increase in property tax (ozb) is inevitable. We must prepare for significant cuts from the national government (Ravijn year). Moreover, GB believes we should be proud of the facilities we have that maintain well-being, social cohesion, and livability.

126131.explanation: The development of business parks should be viewed regionally or even provincially in context. As GB, we see opportunities in knowledge-intensive innovative manufacturing and technology companies. A strong economy and employment keep the municipality of Voorst attractive to live, work, and thrive.

126133.explanation: GB finds this statement polarizing. Of course, GB sees that young people hardly find housing. But in a solidary society, no one sleeps under a bridge. People with urgent housing problems (status holders, divorced parents + children) generally always get priority in allocation.

126135.explanation: GB is positive about farmers switching from traditional farming to other forms of entrepreneurship, such as energy farming. Banning solar panels on agricultural land hinders entrepreneurship. GB wants solar parks in strategic locations and neatly integrated.

126137.explanation: This is already possible. In the Netherlands, there is freedom of education. Lessons in Low Saxon logically do not occur because the pressure on our teachers is already extremely high. GB will always advocate continuing to invest in basic skills like reading, writing, and arithmetic.

126138.explanation: GB sees, after the legal drinking age was raised from 16 to 18, an increase in soft drug use among our youth. We do not want to further stimulate this by allowing coffee shops.

126140.explanation: GB wants to build according to need with attention to the environment. We are positive about taller buildings in Twello village center. On the other hand, GB believes Voorst should be complementary to Apeldoorn, Deventer, and Zutphen, where tall residential towers are more appropriate. This promotes housing flow.

126143.explanation: GB believes that making Twello center car-free infringes on the mobility of, for example, elderly people with difficulty walking. Moreover, Twello center is attractive to people from Apeldoorn, Deventer, and Zutphen who come by car. GB is in favor of further reducing car traffic in the center.

126144.explanation: The local tax burden in Voorst is relatively high. One reason is the high level of facilities. As GB, we have always stood for this and will continue to do so. GB advocates for crucial facilities, especially in the small villages.

126146.explanation: GB is distinctly the sports party of the municipality of Voorst. Unlike grass fields, artificial turf allows year-round sports. GB therefore wants to explore providing municipal loans to sports clubs. This enables clubs to borrow money at a low interest rate.

126090.explanation: CDA Voorst stands for equal opportunities for everyone but opposes strict quotas. For us, what matters is that leaders are chosen based on quality, trust, and support, as human beings.

126091.explanation: Municipal council members represent the voters and are accountable.

126093.explanation: CDA Voorst disagrees with this statement in principle. CDA Voorst believes that fees should be cost-covering and fair. This does not exclude possible increases.

126094.explanation: CDA Voorst is positive about side activities at farms, but not always and everywhere. It must fit with the landscape, environment, and livability.

126097.explanation: CDA Voorst supports the Spreading Act. As a result, it is logical that Voorst makes an equitable contribution to the reception of asylum seekers.

126102.explanation: A central fireworks show can be unifying but is not a necessity. Safety, costs, and public support are factors to consider.

126103.explanation: This is not realistic due to the costs.

126104.explanation: Festivals and village parties are of great value for the livability and connectedness in our villages. The current noise standards provide a careful balance between enjoyment and tranquility. Stricter enforcement can already greatly help prevent nuisance.

126110.explanation: Music education contributes to creativity, development, and enjoyment. The subsidy should help ensure all children have access to music education. Funds are available for this in the municipality of Voorst.

126112.explanation: Monuments tell the story of our villages and landscape and contribute to identity, tourism, and livability. CDA Voorst believes monument care should not be an afterthought. By targeted investment and supporting owners, monuments remain preserved for future generations.

126113.explanation: Safety starts with visibility and approachability. CDA Voorst believes extra enforcement officers should be deployable when necessary for traffic safety, livability, and preventing nuisance.

126114.explanation: CDA Voorst does not see camera surveillance as a standard solution but as a targeted tool to increase safety for specific problems like vandalism or nuisance.

126119.explanation: CDA Voorst believes holiday homes are intended for recreation, not permanent residence. Permanent residence puts pressure on facilities, enforcement, and the rural area, and undermines the tourist and landscape character of our municipality.

126120.explanation: The CDA Voorst wants to protect the green and village identity of our municipality. Infill development is therefore preferred. At the same time, we recognize that the housing shortage sometimes requires carefully chosen expansions at the edges of villages. This can only be done on a small scale and well integrated into the landscape.

126121.explanation: CDA Voorst believes in participation according to ability. A reciprocal obligation can contribute to structure, self-esteem, and involvement in society. At the same time, customization is essential: not everyone can do the same and no one should be overburdened. The goal is activation and perspective, not punishment or coercion.

126123.explanation: CDA Voorst believes that we should align with national trends in this regard.

126125.explanation: Electric driving requires sufficient charging options. CDA Voorst believes that additional charging points may be necessary, especially in residential neighborhoods where people do not have their own driveway. At the same time, we must fairly allocate scarce parking space.

126126.explanation: CDA Voorst values humane care, in which respect is given to a person's background and beliefs. At the same time, care must remain available, affordable, and feasible. However, we do not want to return to pillarization, also because this falls outside the municipal domain.

126127.explanation: Youth care is about quality, continuity, and trust, not just the lowest price.

126128.explanation: CDA Voorst believes that residents and entrepreneurs should not automatically be the budget's residual item. However, this may be inevitable when municipal services come under pressure.

126131.explanation: CDA Voorst sees that local entrepreneurs need space to grow and become more sustainable. Limited expansion of business parks may be necessary to preserve employment in our municipality.

126133.explanation: CDA Voorst believes that people with a residence permit should be given perspective and a good start. Housing is necessary for this. At the same time, we must do justice to local home seekers who often have been waiting a long time.

126135.explanation: CDA Voorst wants to handle both the landscape and the energy transition carefully. A complete ban goes too far. We are very cautious with solar panels on agricultural land and give priority to roofs, farmyards, business parks, and infrastructure.

126137.explanation: Low Saxon is part of the identity and history of our region. CDA Voorst finds it valuable that children can get to know it, for example through projects, stories, or guest lessons.

126138.explanation: CDA Voorst believes that a coffeeshop does not fit the character and scale of our municipality. The potential risks to safety, health, and livability outweigh the benefits.

126140.explanation: CDA Voorst is of the opinion that the number of residential floors should fit the environment. For our municipality, this usually means a limited number.

126143.explanation: CDA Voorst supports maintaining the current situation. The center must remain accessible to the elderly, disabled, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

126144.explanation: Facilities such as schools, shops, healthcare, sports, and meeting places are crucial for the livability of our villages. CDA Voorst believes the municipality should play an active and supportive role in this, together with residents, volunteers, and entrepreneurs.

126146.explanation: Artificial turf can contribute to better utilization of sports facilities and continuity of training and matches. The initiative lies with the clubs.

126090.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. Such a government represents all perspectives to make fair decisions. For us, this goes beyond just male and female.

126091.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue is very important, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to assist the municipal council in decision-making. This way we involve people in local democracy.

126093.explanation: No one wants to raise prices lightly. We want fair costs for our residents so that expenses made by an individual are not simply paid by society at large.

126094.explanation: We find it very important that farmers can develop additional activities. This provides extra income and makes Voorst attractive for recreation. But not every activity fits every location.

126097.explanation: We must offer a safe place to people who are fleeing. Additionally, we must show solidarity with other municipalities that already accommodate many. The shelter must fit into the environment.

126098.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure these workers have decent housing without burdening our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

126102.explanation: Voorst is a small municipality and can enjoy the shows organized by Deventer, Apeldoorn, and Zutphen. A drone show would be really cool.

126103.explanation: The polluter pays. This encourages people to think about their waste. The municipality must facilitate curbside collection. This avoids hassle and results in clean streets.

126104.explanation: We want to limit nuisance for residents nearby and prevent hearing damage.

126110.explanation: We believe that music lessons should be a fixed part of the curriculum, preferably in cooperation with local music associations.

126112.explanation: Our monuments make our municipality beautiful, unique, and attractive to tourists. We believe they should be well cared for. It would be a tremendous shame if unique places are lost forever because we choose to cut back now.

126113.explanation: We consider it important that if we have rules, they are also enforced. Public order officers help with this enforcement.

126114.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

126119.explanation: This is not a permanent solution. It harms recreation. Experiences in other municipalities show that this quickly leads to undermining activities.

126120.explanation: The green character of our municipality is very important. But by building directly adjacent to existing villages, we can preserve the open character. Additionally, we support redevelopment within villages while preserving greenery.

126121.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. We find the exchange (reciprocal obligation) far too coercive. The municipality should support finding activities that suit them.

126123.explanation: We believe the municipality should consider individual circumstances and offer generous help. A strict income limit does not fit this.

126125.explanation: More and more people are driving electric cars. There will be a growing need for charging points.

126126.explanation: When you need care, you are in a vulnerable position and it gives peace of mind to receive care from someone who matches your faith, culture, or conviction.

126127.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

126128.explanation: No one wants to raise taxes easily. So we won't do this without clear reasons for the money. If necessary, we apply our principle 'the strongest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens'.

126131.explanation: Local entrepreneurs are asking for the ability to expand. This offers opportunities to establish sustainable, small-scale sites. This creates space to relocate businesses from the center and keep heavy traffic out of villages.

126133.explanation: There are far too few social rental homes and many groups need priority for various reasons, for example divorced couples. Status holders are a relatively small group here. The municipality currently places several status holders in one building.

126135.explanation: We must start by fully utilizing suitable roofs. If a good initiative comes from residents to collectively establish a solar field, we are not opposed to it.

126137.explanation: Low Saxon is more than a language. It defines the culture, tradition, and history of this region. Therefore, it is an important part of broader cultural education. The Tinus van 't Slyck route is a great example of this.

126138.explanation: There is sufficient supply in Deventer, Apeldoorn, and Zutphen.

126140.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit the character of our villages. Apartment buildings up to and including four floors are fine.

126143.explanation: There is enough parking space around the center and you can have large groceries delivered. A car-free Village Square makes the center much more livable, safer, and attractive.

126144.explanation: Good facilities keep a village livable. Think of the school, meeting places, general practitioner, and supermarket. By building more in and around the village, the demand for these facilities naturally increases, ensuring they remain viable.

126146.explanation: This is a matter for the sports club. The municipality can support, for example with a low-interest loan and by entering into long-term rental contracts. This gives the club certainty that the investment is worthwhile.

126090.explanation: D66 wants an executive board with a balanced male-female distribution because good governance should reflect the entire society.

126091.explanation: We want to increase the voter's influence by introducing a binding corrective referendum. This allows residents to reverse decisions if they feel politics have drifted too far from their will. Additionally, new forms of participation like citizen assemblies are also encouraged by D66.

126093.explanation: Permit fees must cover their costs. The municipality should neither make a profit nor a loss from them.

126094.explanation: It is a positive development if farms want to further develop, but under the conditions set by the municipality, for example regarding size, terraces, and noise.

126097.explanation: The municipality of Voorst has not yet taken its responsibility around asylum, and D66 wants to make up for this. This can be done by establishing a reception center, but also through other means.

126098.explanation: Employers must provide housing, but not on their own property as that is not feasible.

126102.explanation: From 2026 onwards, there will be a fireworks ban. The municipality can offer a great alternative in the form of a fireworks show.

126103.explanation: Free collection can remove barriers to throwing away items instead of reusing or repairing them.

126104.explanation: The progress already made by the municipality of Voorst is sufficient for D66 Voorst.

126110.explanation: D66 Voorst wants to stimulate music at school with a subsidy; by doing this through music associations, we also support them.

126113.explanation: We focus on youth work instead of enforcement officers (boa's).

126119.explanation: When there is a need for it. It helps to solve the housing crisis.

126120.explanation: There are development opportunities for new construction even in the rural area that we can utilize.

126121.explanation: People on social assistance should primarily participate in society; this does not always have to be paid work.

126125.explanation: Tasks still remain especially outside the Twello core.

126126.explanation: Free choice of care is important, but attention to affordability, quality, and effectiveness of care is more important.

126127.explanation: Qualitatively good care ultimately yields more (both financially and substantively for the youth).

126131.explanation: If the opportunity arises, D66 Voorst is willing to explore the possibilities.

126133.explanation: Status holders are one of the urgent groups who should be able to get priority for housing. This also applies to people with a medical indication, divorced parents with children, or people leaving institutions.

126135.explanation: We must consider whether the agricultural land and the environment are suitable for this.

126137.explanation: The opportunity must be available for schools that want it, for example by offering a curriculum package.

126140.explanation: If it fits into the landscape.

126143.explanation: Not the entire center, but a limited part.

126144.explanation: The municipality of Voorst already does a lot and must continue to do so. It does not always have to be financial.

126090.explanation: We leave the equal number of men and women open. We value quality and don’t mind if it consists of 80 percent women and 20 percent men.

126091.explanation: Through participation but not by referendum.

126094.explanation: It must be appropriate for the environment.

126097.explanation: We find a large asylum seekers center too big for our municipality. We advocate for small-scale shelter in Twello and surrounding villages.

126098.explanation: It does not necessarily have to be on their own property, but housing must be provided.

126102.explanation: You can better use this money elsewhere.

126104.explanation: Many people complain about this and experience nuisance.

126110.explanation: Schools can finance this themselves from their lump sum budget.

126112.explanation: Our heritage is precious to us.

126114.explanation: At dark spots, this is necessary.

126119.explanation: Temporary given the severe housing shortage. For example, five years.

126120.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

126123.explanation: In some cases this already happens, for example with energy. Applying tailor-made solutions in special cases.

126125.explanation: That seems like a good idea to us.

126126.explanation: We completely agree with that. Care that fits the identity of the client is an important factor.

126127.explanation: Also paying attention to the price. Especially the effectiveness of the assistance.

126133.explanation: This is already arranged by the national government.

126135.explanation: No solar panels on fertile agricultural land.

126137.explanation: We as a faction are divided on this...

126138.explanation: No cannabis sales in our municipality.

126143.explanation: Many short trips are done by car. Shops must be easily accessible for customers.

126144.explanation: The municipality already does a lot in cooperation with the village interest associations.

126146.explanation: No artificial turf. This is the responsibility of the sports clubs.

126090.explanation: As a municipality we want to be a reflection of society. So diversity is important to us. But diversity is much more than just sex and gender. Ultimately, a person's qualities and motivation to make a difference for our residents are most important.

126091.explanation: We want to experiment with new forms of democracy by actively involving residents in new policies/projects, decision-making, and (local) politics. We would also like to investigate the possible pros and cons of a municipal referendum.

126093.explanation: Permits must remain affordable for residents, associations, and entrepreneurs. Increasing fees should not become a barrier to building, starting a business, or launching initiatives. However, we find it reasonable that fees are indexed and that cost recovery is considered.

126094.explanation: We support the promotion of locally produced food and the sale of local products. This is a way to move towards more sustainable food production. We also like to support good initiatives in the area of (farmer) campsites.

126097.explanation: We do not have suitable buildings for an asylum seekers' center (AZC), and there is insufficient support for it. We prefer to focus on status holders, especially families who can be well dispersed across the villages. This creates space in AZCs elsewhere and also complies with the Distribution Act.

126098.explanation: We consider it especially important that seasonal workers have decent housing, where it matters less whether this is on the company premises or elsewhere. There must be attention to (possible) nuisance, fire safety, and prevention of exploitation.

126102.explanation: A nationwide fireworks ban is coming. If laws and regulations permit, the municipality facilitates that fireworks shows can be organized. The municipality doesn’t have to do this itself; this could also be arranged by associations, villages, or local communities.

126103.explanation: We are in favor of keeping the grey container. No mandatory delivery of residual waste to (underground) containers. And it must be as easy as possible to deliver/bulk waste disposal. This prevents illegal dumping in nature or on agricultural land.

126104.explanation: Festivals and parties bring residents together. We welcome this. We do think there should be clear frameworks aiming for a balance between festivities and livability (preventing nuisance).

126110.explanation: Music lessons are fun and valuable for children's development. We are especially strong supporters of (more) music at school. We provide subsidies to music associations. This way, every child gets to know art and culture.

126112.explanation: The municipality of Voorst is rich in cultural history. We commit ourselves to preserving and restoring the cultural-historical environment. Heritage and monuments are well maintained so they can be passed on to younger generations. Owners and the municipality must work together in this.

126113.explanation: We want to deploy an extra public order and safety officer (boa). Capacity is currently (too) limited, resulting in insufficient enforcement. With an additional boa, traffic measures can be better enforced and visibility in the villages and rural areas will increase.

126114.explanation: It can be useful in places with many incidents, but we do not want the entire municipality covered with cameras. Careful consideration is needed about where cameras are really necessary, with attention to safety and privacy.

126119.explanation: Due to a severe shortage of housing (especially affordable housing), we believe permanent residence in holiday homes should be allowed under conditions.

126120.explanation: Housing construction is our priority, preferably for our own residents. Additionally, we welcome adaptation of the existing housing stock, for example through subdivision or adding storeys. This should happen within village boundaries if possible (infill), but new construction in rural areas should also be possible (expansion).

126121.explanation: We are moderately in favor. Social work, such as volunteer work, can help people stay active, connect with others, and participate in society. But it must fit someone's situation, offer perspective, and not displace paid work. This is more tailor-made.

126123.explanation: We have a social heart and find it important that everyone can continue to participate. For the real low-income groups there are subsidies and funds like Stichting Leergeld, Stichting Pepernoot, and the food bank. For our residents who earn just a bit more, an indexation is the least that can be done.

126125.explanation: Extra charging points are fine, but only if there is a real need. As long as it is unclear whether we have too few and thus lack a solid basis, we should not give up regular parking spaces. Most people do not drive electric cars.

126126.explanation: We are fairly neutral on this: care must be accessible and of good quality for everyone. Personal preferences are important but should not hinder availability and affordability of care.

126127.explanation: Price must certainly weigh heavily to keep control over rising youth care costs (and thus the municipal budget), but “doing what is necessary” remains leading: we always look at what the resident really needs and ensure appropriate help is central.

126128.explanation: We choose to increase burdens only if it really cannot be avoided. First look at the municipality’s own expenditures and don’t too easily pass the bill to residents via property tax (OZB). We find indexation reasonable, but we have difficulty with OZB increases of more than 20 percent proposed by other parties.

126131.explanation: The municipality of Voorst is centrally located and easily accessible. We choose growth of business activity in logical locations such as Engelenburg, De Kar, and along the A1. By concentrating businesses, we strengthen the economy, limit nuisance, and create space for housing and sustainability.

126133.explanation: Due to the distribution act, housing is needed, but we do not want to reduce the existing housing supply. Therefore, we opt for extra flexible homes for status holders so that local housing seekers are not pushed further back.

126135.explanation: The municipality of Voorst is a green municipality with lots of nature and farmland, and that should remain so. As long as there is ample space for solar panels on roofs of homes and businesses, we avoid nature and agricultural land as much as possible. We are against large solar fields on (fertile) agricultural land.

126137.explanation: We like to keep the regional language visible and audible. For example, with an annual Week of the Regional Language, projects in schools, and support for initiatives that strengthen our Low Saxon culture. A council meeting in regional language? We would gladly give it a try!

126138.explanation: We consider prevention and tackling health issues important and encourage a healthy lifestyle. It would therefore be strange to allow a coffeeshop here. Those who want one can visit the existing coffeeshops in neighboring municipalities.

126140.explanation: Young or old, first-time buyer or mover-up: everyone should find a suitable, affordable home. We build with respect for the village/rural character. High residential towers do not fit, but somewhat taller buildings than currently present are possible, for example in some village centers.

126143.explanation: The center should be car-restricted in some places, so residents can shop safely. At the same time, shops must remain easily accessible for everyone, so the center stays lively and attractive.

126144.explanation: We put great effort into preserving facilities in the villages and hamlets that are important for our residents. Think of the general practitioner, pharmacy, primary school, shops (supermarket, hairdresser, etc.), church, sports and music clubs, carnival association, library, public transport, swimming pool, but also the waste containers.

126146.explanation: We want to support sports clubs so they can remain vital associations. We also help where needed in modernizing/sustainability improvements of accommodations and fields. Possibly also for artificial turf fields (although grass is often preferred).

parties.name: VVD Voorst
parties.fullName: Volkspartij voor Vrijheid en Democratie Voorst

126090.explanation: VVD Voorst supports a balance between men and women. The term "must be equal" is too strong. It’s about the quality of the administrators.

126091.explanation: VVD Voorst believes that participation and input in the municipality of Voorst are well organized. Every Voorst resident has ample opportunities to think along and speak up (participation process, roundtable discussions).

126093.explanation: Fees are reviewed annually. In principle, fees should cover the cost of providing the permit; it is certainly not the intention for the municipality of Voorst to profit from fees. VVD Voorst will closely monitor the development of fees.

126094.explanation: VVD Voorst is in favor of allowing Voorst farmers to develop secondary activities, provided these activities are appropriate and assessed within the Environmental Law.

126097.explanation: VVD Voorst believes we must be social towards those who truly need help, but certainly also towards our own population. We must comply with the distribution law, no more than that. This can be done in various ways and does not necessarily mean a large asylum seekers center.

126098.explanation: The housing of seasonal workers must be properly and safely arranged in professional facilities. The employer is responsible, and the implementation must take place according to applicable policy in the municipality of Voorst.

126102.explanation: VVD Voorst sees room for more traditional carbide shooting, partly in the context of social cohesion.

126103.explanation: VVD Voorst believes a contribution should be requested for garbage collection, as nothing is free. Voorst residents can dispose of bulky waste at designated locations, also against a normal fee.

126104.explanation: The current standards are sufficient according to VVD Voorst. At a festival, short-term noise within guidelines must be possible. The municipality of Voorst has demonstrated good monitoring and enforcement (e.g., Ground Zero).

126110.explanation: VVD Voorst values culture, including music lessons. However, there are sufficient possibilities and funds for schools to request support for this outside the municipality. Subsidy from the municipality of Voorst is not necessary.

126112.explanation: VVD Voorst is proud of the monuments in the municipality of Voorst. It acknowledges that more-than-average monuments are present in Voorst. As long as the budget permits, VVD Voorst does not support cuts.

126113.explanation: VVD Voorst places high importance on safety. With the growing number of residents and the transition of the countryside (vacant or rented barns), VVD Voorst believes more attention is needed for safety through enforcement.

126114.explanation: VVD Voorst values safety highly. Camera surveillance can contribute to this, while respecting individual freedom and privacy. This is well regulated within existing policy.

126119.explanation: VVD Voorst does not support permanent residence in recreational homes. We do favor high-quality recreational accommodation.

126120.explanation: VVD Voorst supports building in all centers with a focus on infill development. Preserving the green character of the municipality of Voorst is very important. VVD Voorst also sees possibilities at the edges of village boundaries, as long as the plans fit well.

126121.explanation: VVD Voorst believes people should be broadly supported, including social acceptance and connection. Providing a return service, as reasonably possible, gives people the advantage of staying socially involved, even when times are tough.

126123.explanation: VVD Voorst supports annually monitoring the income threshold. People who truly need it receive the right form of support.

126125.explanation: VVD Voorst believes there should be enough publicly accessible parking spaces with charging capacity, matching the growth of electric mobility. A concrete inventory of needs has been made in recent years, and we build on that.

126126.explanation: VVD Voorst believes that as many individual choice options as possible should be available. However, this is largely outside the direct reach of the municipality.

126127.explanation: VVD Voorst considers the best balance between result, quality, and price most important. Through regional cooperation, the municipality of Voorst can procure the best youth care.

126128.explanation: The property tax (OZB) was recently strongly increased (10%) by a majority in the municipal council. VVD Voorst does not support this. Balancing income and expenses is the motto. Homeowners are not infinite money trees.

126131.explanation: VVD Voorst supports expansion at Engelenburg, which is already underway. It is good for the economy that space for entrepreneurs is created. This contributes to employment and revenues (taxes, land sales).

126133.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation to provide housing for status holders, not necessarily their own social housing. VVD Voorst supports making a maximum of 50 percent of available capacity available to local residents, according to legal limits.

126135.explanation: VVD Voorst supports preserving valuable agricultural land and strongly prefers solar panels on roofs. We also support setting up Energy Hubs. VVD Voorst does not support very large wind turbines.

126137.explanation: VVD Voorst finds it important to pay attention to local culture. Low Saxon is part of this. However, schools decide their own curriculum.

126138.explanation: VVD Voorst does not support establishing a coffeeshop.

126140.explanation: VVD Voorst sees room for taller apartment complexes in the center of Twello. This allows more people to have access to housing. It is important that this development fits the environment, complies with the Environmental Law, and provides sufficient parking.

126143.explanation: Twello-Center must remain accessible, especially for the disabled or people with limited mobility. One option is to redesign the through connection between Oonk and Waardige Waard so that Twello-Center becomes more car-restricted without sacrificing accessibility.

126144.explanation: VVD Voorst supports facilities in the centers. Collaboration between village associations and village aldermen has contributed to better insight into needs and possible translation into practical opportunities.

126146.explanation: VVD Voorst prefers traditional grass. It is valued more by athletes, causes fewer injuries, and is much less harmful to the environment. If a sports club decides otherwise, the municipality can facilitate necessary loans.

126090.explanation: The best person in the right place.

126091.explanation: Residents should be heard on essential issues such as a municipal reorganization.

126093.explanation: These costs have increased considerably in recent years. From a liberal perspective, we welcome a reduction in local rules and permits. This saves costs on the civil service and gives more room for initiators.

126094.explanation: Our principle is 'yes, provided'. We encourage local initiatives from entrepreneurs. Agricultural entrepreneurs are looking for new revenue models. Besides their primary role as food producers, farmers also have a social function.

126097.explanation: The national mandate requires us to accommodate asylum seekers. We support early participation with our residents in housing initiatives. We are in favor of small-scale forms of shelter and against an asylum seekers' center location.

126098.explanation: But our policies must also support the entrepreneurs here.

126102.explanation: We want to facilitate local initiatives from all villages and neighborhoods. No restriction to only one show per municipality. This strengthens social cohesion in our municipality.

126103.explanation: First, we want to maintain the current system with containers at home. In addition, it is good to investigate collecting bulky waste at home once a year.

126104.explanation: The current regulations are sufficiently strict.

126110.explanation: This is the responsibility of the schools. We are positive about music lessons but do not see this as an additional task for the municipality.

126112.explanation: Yes, this is mainly because more and more municipal monuments are being designated. That also requires a lot of administrative capacity/costs.

126113.explanation: We find it important that our residents have a fixed contact person/neighborhood officer. For reports/emergencies, we believe there should be enforcement officers available for a quick response.

126114.explanation: Opposed due to privacy concerns and the further digitalization of society and control pressure from the government.

126119.explanation: Yes, a temporary exemption of, for example, '10 years' is a suitable solution in the current situation/housing crisis.

126120.explanation: Our starting point is that there is space on existing plots and by splitting existing homes/buildings. Infill development takes precedence over expansion. The large housing task sometimes also requires expansion locations near existing villages.

126121.explanation: We encourage everyone to participate in society, and volunteer work is important to also reintegrate into the labor process. Encourage where possible; mandatory measures do not work.

126125.explanation: If there is more need, the municipality should facilitate that.

126126.explanation: Care is independent of religion, culture, and beliefs.

126127.explanation: Both price and quality are important; price can never be the sole leading factor.

126128.explanation: No, the property tax has already been exorbitantly increased as of 2026, and we opposed this. We advocate for a reduction of the property tax.

126131.explanation: Yes, provided there is a need and it fits the environment.

126133.explanation: No, priority for status holders is undesirable because local housing seekers are increasingly disadvantaged.

126135.explanation: Valuable agricultural land must be preserved for food production. We primarily see solar panels on roofs and on industrial sites/unused sites.

126137.explanation: 'Must' is not the right approach. It would be nice if attention is given to this because it is part of our culture and identity.

126138.explanation: If this need exists, a location could be facilitated for that purpose.

126140.explanation: High residential towers do not fit in our living environment.

126143.explanation: No, this actually makes the center of Twello attractive to shoppers.

126144.explanation: By adding homes in all villages, amenities such as schools and shops can remain for longer.

126146.explanation: If this investment results in broader usability of the fields for sports, schools, and other community activities.

statements.title: The municipality should do more to maintain facilities in the villages.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer doen om

titleParts.glossary: Buildings or services needed for daily life, such as roads, sports facilities or healthcare.

titleParts.text: in de dorpen in stand te houden.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Voorst should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The prices of municipal permits must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The residents of Voorst should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Voorst moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in the municipality of Voorst.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Voorst.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include Low Saxon.
More business parks should be developed in Voorst.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The center of Twello should be made car-free.
The municipality should invest in installing artificial turf fields at sports clubs.
